thresholds.title: Locked Room
thresholds.text: In the early years, memory was a burden pressed between the walls. Every night had the same rhythm: the yank of curtains, a breath in the crack of the door, a voicelessness full of expectation. In the locked room, the concept of choice did not exist. There was only listening. When God remained silent, loneliness was drawn into the throat. You felt your heart beating from fear, and your body reacting before you could think.

thresholds.title: First Exit
thresholds.text: Returning to freedom did not mean the end of trauma. You left physically, but your body still carried the echo of the locked room. The same words that broke you before now came back as quiet blows — thin comments, judgment, reminders of a debt of gratitude. In your head, the boundaries between safe space and minefield were blurred.

thresholds.title: Pandemic Rest
thresholds.text: Outside, the pandemic became a justification for your fear, and inside — a rare moment of breath. Long months of hiding behind masks, shutdowns, and empty streets restored the silence you knew from the locked room, but now it wore a different face. The fear that once seemed exaggerated received social validation. At last, you no longer had to pretend you were calm.

thresholds.title: Second Exit
thresholds.text: The death of your father and mother meant the end of their present shadow, but it did not wash away the imprints they left behind. In that moment, a mixture of relief and disorientation emerged. The body exhaled, but the voice of the past still whispered that the threat could return. In the silence after your parents, a different path opened: which part of you has the right to feel relief, and which part is still busy preparing for pain.

thresholds.title: Shadow Loop
thresholds.text: Homes disappeared from your map, but your organism never got the message. The sound of a closing door, a breath behind your back, even an unexpected tone of voice — they can transport you back to that night in a second. Then you are a child in the locked room again, even though physically you are far away and you know the threat has passed. Shadow Loop is the day when dreams from the past looped into the present.

thresholds.title: Third Exit
thresholds.text: Here the deepest sleep ends. Your consciousness begins to understand that the war that raged within your nerves has truly been concluded. This is not triumph through endurance — it is the conscious acceptance that the threat exists only as an echo now, and that you can place a barrier before it. The awakening is newly rough and stable. You are learning to build your right to defend yourself not as revenge, but as healing.

ending: Freedom does not always sound like an exit. Sometimes it is a brief interruption — a moment when you finally hear only your own breath, and not his.
